titleStmt.title: SII 9.42: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 42. (A.R. No. 64 of 1904.) ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE KALLESVARA TEMPLE AT KALKAMBHA, BELLARY TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.

summary.p: This is dated Śaka 949, Prabhava, Chaitra, śu. 1, Bṛihaspativāra. In Prabhava= Śaka 949 expired, Chaitra Śu. 1 fell on Sunday, not Thursday as stated. But in the next year i.e. Vibhava the tithi cited did fall on Thursday = 29th February A.D. 1028; this was probably the date intended. The record refers itself to the reign of the Noḻamba-Pallava king, Jagadēkamalla-Noḻambapallava-Permānaḍidēva ruling from Vipranārāyaṇa. A certain Sahale-Gavuḍa is said to have made a grant of some land for the service of the god Mahādēva at Chihanapaṭṭi. The record is damaged at the end.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . samadhigatapaṁcamahāsabda pa
llavānvaya śrīprithvīvalla
bhe pallavakuḷatiḷakanamōghavākhya
kāṁcīpuravarēśvaraṁ śrīmajjagadēkama
llanoḻaṁbapallava permmānaḍidēva
r vipranārāyaṇada nelevīḍinalsu
khasankathāvinōdadiṁ rājyamutta
rōttarābhivri(r)ddhipravardhamānamā
caṁdrārkatāraṁbaraṁ saluttami
re sakhavarṣa 949 neya pra
bhavasaṁvatsarada caitra suddha 1 ne
brihaspativāradaṁdu cihana
paṭṭiya mūlasthānada mahā
dēvarge dharmmapratipāḷanaṁgeyda
vṛtti sahalegavuḍa biṭṭa yereya ma
tta 13 yavaścaṁdraśca suryya
śca tāvattiṣṭhati mēdinī yāvat
makathā lōkē taddharmma vidhīya
tē .
Digital edition of SII 9.42 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
